antiReferences.name: Gym-bro protein RTD
antiReferences.why: Too performance-led, heavy, disciplined, and macro-first. PEP should not feel like a post-workout obligation or bodybuilding product.

antiReferences.name: drinkpep.com
antiReferences.why: Same-name collision with a different US energy/probiotic product. Do not borrow its claims, audience, or identity cues.

antiReferences.name: Clinical supplement brands
antiReferences.why: Too medicinal and rational. PEP needs wellness credibility without becoming technical or prescriptive.

antiReferences.name: Energy drink brands
antiReferences.why: Too stimulant-led, intense, and performance-coded for the Lifestyle upgrade direction.

antiReferences.name: Meal replacement shakes
antiReferences.why: Too heavy and utility-driven. PEP should feel light, refreshing, social, and treat-like.

antiReferences.name: Party-first mocktail or spirit brands
antiReferences.why: Useful as context, but they push PEP toward the Better social drink direction rather than everyday wellness lifestyle.

audience: Wellness-focused consumers who want healthier choices without effort or sacrifice.
Identity-driven drink buyers who want the brand in their hand to reflect their lifestyle.
Café, coworking, beach, and wellness-retail customers seeking a refreshing treat with a better nutritional profile.
People replacing sugary soft drinks, heavy smoothies, snacks, or routine café drinks.
Social/out-of-home consumers who want something light, premium, and feel-good without a gym or nightlife frame.
brandFitRules: Accept ideas that make PEP feel like wellness without sacrifice: refreshing, tasty, light, and easy to choose.
Lead with lifestyle, taste, and occasion; keep protein as the credible benefit, not the whole personality.
Reject anything that feels gym-first, performance-first, macro-first, or supplement-like.
Reject anything that feels mainly like a cocktail replacement, bar ritual, festival drink, or alcohol alternative.
Use brunch, coworking, beach, wellness retail, and afternoon refreshment as the core occasion filter.
Keep copy friendly, relaxed, and optimistic; never disciplinary, clinical, or transformational.
Make nutrition simple and believable; do not over-explain or over-claim.
Design direction should be spacious, minimal, soft in energy, and premium-but-approachable, without becoming vague.
Do not copy competitor logos, distinctive packaging systems, or signature assets.
Always distinguish PEP at pep-drink.com from drinkpep.com.

designPatterns: Wellness benefit is easy to understand.
Taste and lifestyle cues stay prominent.
Playful but still adult enough for wellness retail.
Strong flavour-led shelf logic without a supplement feel.
inLineBrands.drinkTypeFit: Strong lifestyle beverage peer; not protein, but directly relevant as a healthier refreshing drink replacement.
inLineBrands.name: BLOOM
inLineBrands.why: Sets a clear precedent for a wellness-oriented drink that feels fun, refreshing, and easy to adopt as part of everyday lifestyle.

designPatterns: Health benefit framed through approachable refreshment.
Everyday treat positioning with better-for-you credibility.
Friendly brand world rather than technical nutrition framing.
inLineBrands.drinkTypeFit: Strong lifestyle drink peer; direct competitor for healthier soda occasions, though not protein-based.
inLineBrands.name: OLIPOP
inLineBrands.why: A strong benchmark for turning a functional ingredient story into a lifestyle-friendly soda replacement with broad appeal.

designPatterns: High social shareability without becoming nightlife-led.
Clear flavour variety as a repeat-purchase driver.
inLineBrands.drinkTypeFit: Strong lifestyle drink peer; direct competitor for healthier everyday refreshment, though not protein-based.
inLineBrands.name: Poppi
inLineBrands.why: Shows how a wellness drink can feel culturally current, taste-led, and identity-driven.

designPatterns: Wellness and craft cues without harshness.
Taste differentiation through smoothness and balance.
inLineBrands.drinkTypeFit: Strong lifestyle/wellness beverage peer; kombucha is a direct competitor in healthy refreshment moments.
inLineBrands.name: Alive & Well
inLineBrands.why: Fits PEP’s need to make wellness feel accessible, refreshing, and more pleasant than expected.

designPatterns: Mood and wellness benefit expressed simply.
inLineBrands.drinkTypeFit: Strong lifestyle beverage peer; direct competitor for everyday feel-good refreshment, though not protein-based.
inLineBrands.name: Recess
inLineBrands.why: Highly relevant for a calm, modern wellness drink that behaves like a lifestyle accessory rather than a supplement.

designPatterns: Functional benefit tied to familiar soda refreshment.
inLineBrands.drinkTypeFit: Strong lifestyle/functional soda peer; relevant for healthier everyday refreshment, though not protein-led.
inLineBrands.name: more fiz
inLineBrands.why: Useful benchmark for making functional nutrition feel like an easy, refreshing soft-drink upgrade.

designPatterns: Premium wellness without a sports frame.
inLineBrands.drinkTypeFit: Strong lifestyle/wellness drink peer; kombucha directly competes in healthy refreshment occasions.
inLineBrands.name: Yaya Kombucha
inLineBrands.why: Although unassigned in the source positioning, it strongly fits the Lifestyle upgrade lane through premium kombucha, authenticity, and wellness refreshment.

inLineBrands.drinkTypeFit: Strong lifestyle/wellness drink peer; kombucha and botanical sodas compete in the same better-for-you refreshment set.
inLineBrands.name: Walker Brothers
inLineBrands.why: Although unassigned in the source positioning, it is relevant as a craft wellness drink benchmark with adult flavour complexity and everyday refreshment logic.

layoutOnlyReferences.name: Revized
patterns: Use only for structural inspiration, not brand positioning or identity.
Potentially useful for clear flavour navigation, modular product sections, and simple conversion flow.
Do not borrow its visual identity, tone, or product-world assumptions.

oneLiner: PEP is a premium, light, refreshing protein drink for people who want healthier everyday choices to feel like a treat.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Weak for PEP’s active direction; functional drink category is relevant, but the mindset is too supplement-led.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: HOLY
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs to Functional protein drink / direction 1.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Its focus on functional benefits and protein-rich utility pushes toward gym and nutrition-tool logic rather than effortless lifestyle refreshment.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Weak; hydration/electrolyte mixes are not direct lifestyle treat competitors.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: LMNT
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs to Functional protein drink / direction 1.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Its science-backed electrolyte logic is rational, disciplined, and performance-adjacent, which is not the PEP Lifestyle upgrade mood.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Weak; BCAA/energy drink is too sports and stimulant-coded.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: NOCCO
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs to Functional protein drink / direction 1.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: It is too athletic, energizing, and workout-oriented for PEP’s soft everyday wellness role.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Poor; protein plus alcohol novelty creates the wrong association.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: PROTOCHOL
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs to Functional protein drink / direction 1 with some social novelty overlap.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: It makes protein feel like a gimmick in an alcohol context, while PEP must remain light, healthy, refreshing, and premium.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Adjacent but too functional; hydration is relevant, but the brand logic is benefit-first.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: VITAMIN WELL
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs to Functional protein drink / direction 1.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: It is more about vitamin hydration utility than identity-driven lifestyle upgrade.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Adjacent social beverage, but not direct everyday protein refreshment.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Kin Euphorics
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs to Better social drink / direction 3.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: It is mood-enhancement and social-experience led, which risks pulling PEP away from everyday lifestyle wellness into social ritual territory.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Weak; non-alcoholic spirits are a different use case.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Lyre's
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs to Better social drink / direction 3.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: It is built around alcohol replacement and cocktail occasions, not everyday healthy refreshment.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Weak; non-alcoholic spirits are not a direct PEP category.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Seedlip
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs to Better social drink / direction 3.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Its refined social substitute role is too spirits-led and formal for PEP’s accessible lifestyle habit.

positioningFitTypeMismatch.drinkTypeFit: Moderate; useful soft-drink and premium refreshment benchmark, but more aperitif-led than PEP.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.name: EVER TREE / Everleaf
positioningFitTypeMismatch.positioningMismatch: Same active lifestyle territory, but leans toward adult aperitif and mindful-drinking cues.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.typeMismatch: Aperitif/soft-drink ritual is adjacent; PEP is an everyday protein refreshment, not primarily an aperitif.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.whyBrandingFits: Refined, natural, refreshing, and premium in a way that can inform PEP’s elevated lifestyle feel without copying the occasion.

positioningFitTypeMismatch.drinkTypeFit: Moderate to weak; convenient and lifestyle-led, but cocktail framing is not core to PEP.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.name: Ole cocktails
positioningFitTypeMismatch.positioningMismatch: Same active lifestyle territory in the source, but overlaps with social drinking cues.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.typeMismatch: Cocktail/mocktail format is too close to party and hospitality occasions; PEP must remain a protein-led everyday refreshment.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.whyBrandingFits: Useful for convenience, premium ease, and social lifestyle energy, but should not define PEP’s category frame.

positioningFitTypeMismatch.drinkTypeFit: Weak to moderate; premium alcohol-free lifestyle brand, but not a direct drink-type peer.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.name: Ish
positioningFitTypeMismatch.positioningMismatch: Same active lifestyle territory in the source, but leans toward social substitute rituals.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.typeMismatch: Spirit, wine, and mocktail alternatives are not PEP’s core format; PEP should not be positioned as an alcohol replacement.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.whyBrandingFits: Useful for modern premium lifestyle confidence and inclusivity, but too close to social ritual to be an in-line model.

positioningStatement: PEP is a premium, light, refreshing protein drink for wellness-focused people who want a healthier everyday choice that feels like a treat and fits naturally into brunch, coworking, beach, and afternoon refreshment moments.

visual.designConcept: More minimal and gentle: spacious composition, low clutter, soft energy, and a refined everyday feel. The design should make PEP feel like a lifestyle accessory or habit while keeping the protein benefit clear and credible.
visual.mood: Airy, relaxed, refreshing, premium-but-approachable, unhurried, feel-good, and socially easy.
visual.photography: Real lifestyle moments: café tables, coworking breaks, beach or park refreshment, hands sharing cans, flavour-led close-ups, and casual out-of-home scenes. People should look natural, relaxed, and health-conscious rather than posed like athletes or nightlife talent.
rejectLooks: Overly technical benefit grids as the main impression.
Generic wellness imagery with no taste or occasion specificity.

voice.tone: Friendly, light, confident, relaxed, taste-led, and wellness-aware. Speak like a stylish friend, not a coach, doctor, or supplement label.
